name: Career Discussion for IT Professionals
description:
This is a recurring session for IT professionals to discuss career growth and market strategy. We focus on how to build rare and valuable skills, improve professional standing, and navigate the current job market.

**The Format:**

* **The Concept (5 mins):** One core idea regarding professional development or career strategy.
* **The Discussion (30 mins):** The floor opens for group feedback. We share real-world experiences and peer advice to help everyone improve their career capital.
* **The Next Idea (5 mins):** Voting for the next core idea to discuss.

**Why Join?** The goal is to move beyond just writing code. We talk about what actually works in the industry right now, from technical positioning to performing better in your current role.

**Join us to share your perspective and hear how others are navigating their career paths.**

name: (CS)²AI Online™ Replay: Demystifying Patching in OT Cybersecurity
description:
This is an airing of an event held live in February2025 on the often confusing challenges of patching in ICS/OT environments.
Register now: [https://events.zoom.us/ev/Aovc9dKAz82IX3AwHcdR-FwFgCfLk5FtiO5J7A3YZViGvd_gzzD3~AsnDnSKakLGK9vutxmN6w8sTs2yg9gvzYvK3cWq0kKxLD_ybfZ05UMlDBmqCR9-po9HfB-j6ExhGwIh8JEg8aHrWUw](https://events.zoom.us/ev/Aovc9dKAz82IX3AwHcdR-FwFgCfLk5FtiO5J7A3YZViGvd_gzzD3~AsnDnSKakLGK9vutxmN6w8sTs2yg9gvzYvK3cWq0kKxLD_ybfZ05UMlDBmqCR9-po9HfB-j6ExhGwIh8JEg8aHrWUw)

⚡ **Rolling Power: Cybersecurity of Electric Vehicles & Automation** ⚡
As electric vehicles (EVs) and automation rapidly reshape the future of transportation and energy, cybersecurity has become a critical pillar of resilience and trust. From charging infrastructure and connected fleets to autonomous driving systems and smart grids, each innovation brings both opportunity and new attack surfaces.
Join industry leaders, security experts, and innovators as we explore the evolving threat landscape and solutions for safeguarding EVs, their digital ecosystems, and the infrastructure that powers them.
🔑 **Key Topics Include:**

* Securing EV charging networks and supply chains
* Protecting autonomous and connected vehicle systems
* Cyber-physical risks across smart mobility and energy integration
* Building resilience in transportation infrastructure
* Emerging standards, policies, and global collaboration efforts

Whether you’re in automotive, energy, cybersecurity, or policy, this event is your gateway to understanding the challenges and opportunities driving the next era of mobility security.

Click to register now:
[https://events.zoom.us/ev/Aovc9dKAz82IX3AwHcdR-FwFgCfLk5FtiO5J7A3YZViGvd_gzzD3~AsnDnSKakLGK9vutxmN6w8sTs2yg9gvzYvK3cWq0kKxLD_ybfZ05UMlDBmqCR9-po9HfB-j6ExhGwIh8JEg8aHrWUw](https://events.zoom.us/ev/Aovc9dKAz82IX3AwHcdR-FwFgCfLk5FtiO5J7A3YZViGvd_gzzD3~AsnDnSKakLGK9vutxmN6w8sTs2yg9gvzYvK3cWq0kKxLD_ybfZ05UMlDBmqCR9-po9HfB-j6ExhGwIh8JEg8aHrWUw)

name: OG Coffee&Code Coding!
description:
## ☕ The OG Coffee&Code Meetup

Before the hype. Before the buzzwords. Before “AI-powered everything.”
This is **The OG Code&Coffee** — the meetup that started it all.
No panels. No pitches. No pressure.
Just good people, good coffee, and focused time to **build, learn, and connect**.

### What this meetup is

* An open, welcoming space for **coders, builders, designers, and curious minds**
* A place to **work on your own projects**, homework, startups, side quests, or experiments
* Organic conversations, spontaneous debugging, and genuine community
* Beginner-friendly, expert-respected, ego-free

### What this meetup is *not*

* ❌ A lecture
* ❌ A sales event
* ❌ A recruiting trap
* ❌ A “watch one person talk for 90 minutes” situation

### How it works

* Show up
* Grab coffee ☕
* Open your laptop (or notebook, or brain)
* Work at your own pace
* Chat when you want, focus when you need
* Leave having made progress—or at least a new friend

### Who should come

* Software engineers & data folks
* Students & self-learners
* Founders & indie hackers
* Designers, tinkerers, and technologists
* Anyone who likes building things with other humans

Whether this is your **first Coffee&Code** or you’ve been here since day one—this is the meetup that keeps the spirit alive.
Come for the code.
Stay for the community.
Leave caffeinated ☕🚀

name: PLUG Central - Jitsi Meet: 7-9PM; Speaker from 7:30-8:30PM
description:
Please join us for PLUG Central. During the pandemic we meet on-line at Jitsi Meet, beginning at 7pm. RSVPs are optional. Anyone interested in Linux and Free and Open Source software is welcome to attend. We welcome all levels of expertise, whether you're just curious, a hobbyist, or a professional.

Jitsi Meet agenda:

Q&A with emcee CJ Fearnley from 7-7:30PM. Bring your Linux (and related) questions for a group exploration.

The main presentation from 7:30-8:30PM: TBD.

Q&A continued from 8:30-9PM.

After the pandemic dissipates, we plan to again meet in Lecture Hall A in Griffith Hall at USP in Philadelphia. We plan to revert to the original speaker slot from 8-9PM with Q&A at the beginning to better support travel constraints.

After the presentation some of us adjourn to Clarkville (http://www.clarkvillephilly.com/) a nearby pizza and craft beer restaurant.

University of the Sciences in Philadelphia (USP)
Griffith Hall (Room "A" or "C", look for the signs)
600 South 43rd Street
Philadelphia, PA 19104-4495

USP is located in University City. Driving directions are available at http://www.phillylinux.org/locations/usp.html, or http://www.usip.edu/directions, both of which have an aerial view of the campus buildings. USP is also easily accessible by public transportation.

Often, the door to Griffith Hall is locked at 7PM. If you find the door locked, please contact the security guard (usually in the booth in the parking lot) and tell them that the monthly PLUG (Philadelphia Linux User Group) meeting in Griffith Hall is tonight. If there is no one in the booth you can call UCS's public safety number at 215.895.1117 and they will radio personnel to unlock the door. Our sponsor is Zhiwei Liu in the Chemistry department. Security knows this and will almost always open the door for you and have it unlocked so other participants can enter without issue.

name: (Co)nnect Philly AI Ecosystem Summit
description:
**Join us for (Co)nnect: Philly's AI Ecosystem Summit, a full-day Philly Tech Week gathering designed to bring together the people building, supporting, and growing AI and tech across Philadelphia.**

The idea behind **(Co)** is simple: **community, collaboration, and connection**.
Philadelphia's AI ecosystem is growing quickly. Founders are building. Researchers are innovating. Operators are applying AI in real business settings. Investors, institutions, and ecosystem leaders are helping shape what comes next. What is often missing is shared space to learn from each other, build relationships, and see the full picture more clearly.
That is what **(Co)nnect** is built to do.

This summit brings together the people shaping AI in Philadelphia for a full day of keynotes, fireside conversations, lightning sessions, demos, and networking at Pennovation Works. The goal is to spotlight the strength, creativity, and momentum already present across Philly's AI and tech scene while creating real opportunities for new connection and collaboration.
Whether you are a founder, builder, researcher, investor, operator, student, or simply someone who wants to better understand where AI in Philadelphia is headed, this event is designed to help you connect with the ecosystem in a meaningful way.

### What to expect

At **(Co)nnect**, you can expect:

* A full-day summit during Philly Tech Week
* A strong mix of ecosystem insight and practical learning
* Keynote conversations on Philadelphia's opportunity in AI and tech
* Fireside chats on capital, builders' journeys, talent, infrastructure, and long-term ecosystem growth
* Lightning sessions and demos from people building in the region
* Networking throughout the day with founders, researchers, investors, operators, and community leaders
* A chance to better understand who is shaping Philly's AI future and where new opportunities are emerging

### Basic agenda

**8:30 AM to 9:30 AM**
Arrival, check-in, light refreshments, and networking
**9:30 AM to 10:25 AM**
Opening welcome and keynote
**10:30 AM to 11:20 AM**
Fireside chat and lightning session, round one
**11:30 AM to 12:20 PM**
Fireside chat and lightning session, round two
**12:30 PM to 1:30 PM**
Lunch and networking
**1:30 PM to 2:20 PM**
Fireside chat and lightning session, round three
**2:30 PM to 3:30 PM**
Closing keynote or panel discussion
**3:30 PM to 5:30 PM**
Happy hour, networking, and close

### Who should attend

This event is for people who are building, backing, studying, or shaping the future of AI and technology in Philadelphia, including:

* Founders and startup teams
* AI builders, developers, and technologists
* Researchers and educators
* Investors and innovation leaders
* Operators and enterprise professionals
* Community builders and ecosystem partners
* Students and curious professionals looking to get more connected to Philly's AI scene

### Why attend

If you want a clearer view of Philadelphia's AI ecosystem, stronger local connections, and more insight into where the region is headed next, this is a room worth being in.
You will leave with:

* A better understanding of the people and organizations shaping AI in Philly
* New relationships across sectors and disciplines
* Fresh perspective on where opportunity is growing
* A stronger sense of how you can plug into the ecosystem

### About the venue

**Pennovation Works** is one of Philadelphia's most exciting hubs for innovation and entrepreneurship, making it a fitting home for a summit centered on community, collaboration, and the future of AI in Philly.

### Speaker note

Speaker announcements are coming soon. We are curating a lineup of founders, builders, researchers, investors, operators, and community leaders who reflect the range and momentum of Philadelphia's AI and tech ecosystem.

### Registration

Space is limited, and advance registration is encouraged.
Come be part of the conversations, ideas, and relationships shaping what comes next for AI in Philadelphia.

name: From PRD to Prototype: Hands-On Vibe Coding for the Product Team
description:
**The Problem We're Solving**
Based on Product Tank’s recent survey, most PMs are stuck at "ChatGPT writes my PRD" - only a handful are actually building anything with AI. There's a massive gap between using AI as a writing assistant and using it to create working prototypes.

This workshop bridges that gap. Attendees will go from a Product Requirements Document to a live prototype they built themselves - in less than 90 minutes.

**What Attendees Will Build**
Attendees will pick a project that adds value to their day-to-day. Below is a list of starter options, but feel free to bring your own idea.

1. **Product landing page** — explain a feature or concept, collect interest (featured/recommended)
2. **Interactive prototype** — a clickable multi-step flow or onboarding sequence
3. **User feedback collector** — submit feedback, tag by theme, see it displayed
4. **Feature voting board** — ranked list of ideas your team can interact with

**Every attendee leaves with:**

* A PRD they’ve developed themselves
* A live website URL they can share
* The actual code and files they can modify later
* Proof they can build working prototypes, not just documents

**Why This Approach Works**

**We're teaching real vibe coding** \- the same professional workflow using Claude Code\- not just drag\-and\-drop tools like Webflow or Wix\. This means:

* **Unlimited creative freedom** \- Build anything you can imagine\, not just pre\-built templates
* **Transferable skills** \- Learn professional skills that work for any project

Key insight: **PMs who can prototype their own ideas move faster and communicate more effectively with engineering teams**. This isn't about becoming a developer - it's about removing the dependency on scarce engineering resources for early validation.

**What Attendees Need**

* Laptop (Mac or Windows)
* Wifi/internet connection
* Claude code for desktop
* No coding experience required.

**Quick note:** This is being held at the Pennovation Center during their [(Co)nnect: Philly AI Ecosystem Summit](https://luma.com/xdwih64h). In addition to registering here, be sure to [also register at this link](https://luma.com/xdwih64h) for building access where you can join other AI-related sessions they have during the day.

**Convince the Boss You Should Go!**
Since this is one of our first ProductTank events during workday hours, we wanted to give you a little help if you need to convince the boss. Here are two, almost ready statements you can use on Slack or Email/when you talk to them!

**Manager-Facing (Email or Direct Ask)**
Hi [Manager], I came across a free 90-minute workshop on May 7 called [From PRD to Prototype: Hands-On Vibe Coding for the Product Team](https://www.meetup.com/producttank-philadelphia/events/314163608/), and I’d like to attend. It’s focused on using AI tools to quickly turn product ideas into working prototypes—not just writing PRDs.

This feels especially relevant as we look to move faster on validation and reduce back-and-forth with engineering on early concepts. The session is very hands-on, and I’d leave with a working prototype plus skills I can immediately apply to our current projects. Since it’s free and relatively short, it seems like a high-value way to build capability in an area that’s becoming increasingly important for PMs.

This is exactly the work we want to be doing, so I’d like to use part of my workday on DATE to attend. I’ll come back with a working prototype and new skills to bring back to the team.

**Short Slack / Quick Message Version**
Hey — there’s a free 90-min workshop on May 7th on using AI to turn PRDs into actual working prototypes. It’s very hands-on, and I’d come back with something built + skills we could use right away to speed up early validation.

This is part of my work day and I’ll get us a lot of value back at the office. Any concerns if I block time to attend?

name: Agentic AI Tools in Action
description:
**[Register here to save your spot!](https://ctrly.io/YFbot )**

**Build, deploy, & scale AI agents in *real* w*orkflows*, not just theory.**

This multi-session webinar blends strategic context, live engineering demos, and applied enterprise use cases to show how agentic workflows are designed, deployed, and operated in the real world.

By the end of the workshop, participants will understand how to architect, build, and evaluate agentic AI workflows that integrate with real tools, services, and infrastructure — skills directly applicable to modern production environments.

**What you'll learn**

* How to design agentic workflows that interact with real tools and APIs
* How AI agents change the way software gets built
* Where agents break—and how to work around limitations
* How to evaluate tools like MindStudio for real-world use
* Patterns for integrating agents into existing systems and processes

**Session Highlights**

2:00 PM ET
**The AI Imperative​: Competitive Pressure Meets Implementation Reality​**
A strategic, high‑level exploration of what this moment truly demands from leaders. This session frames AI as a structural transformation that spans strategy, operating models, governance, and value creation.
*Hosted by: Wade Erickson & Richard Taubin (Motion Consulting Group, a Kelly Company)*

2:15 PM ET
**Modernizing Enterprise Systems with Agentic AI**
Explore how organizations are integrating AI agents into existing systems to automate workflows, reduce manual overhead, and unlock new efficiencies—without starting from scratch.
*Hosted by: Amit Tank (Kelly Science, Engineering, Technology & Telecom)*

2:45 PM ET
**The Future of Programming: Building with AI Agents**
AI agents are rapidly changing how software gets built. This session explores this shift, using Remy by MindStudio as a lens into what programming looks like in an agent-driven world. We’ll cover how agents are reshaping development workflows, where they fall short, and what comes next for builders and teams.
*Hosted by: Dmitry Shapiro (MindStudio)*

name: Claude Code Philly
description:
## Claude Code Saturdays 🤖💻

**A weekly hands-on meetup for builders using Claude to write, refactor, and reason about code.**
Claude Code Saturdays is a relaxed, collaborative session for developers, founders, and tinkerers who want to explore what’s possible with Anthropic’s Claude for real-world coding tasks. Whether you’re prototyping a new app, improving an existing codebase, or just experimenting with AI-assisted development, this meetup is a space to learn by doing.

### What to Expect

* 🧑‍💻 **Live coding & pair-building** with Claude
* 🔍 **Prompting techniques** for better code generation and refactoring
* 🧠 **Reasoning-heavy tasks**: debugging, architecture, tests, and docs
* 🚀 **Show & tell**: share what you’re building or stuck on
* ☕ **Casual, friendly atmosphere**—bring your laptop and curiosity

### Who Should Attend

* Software engineers (any level)
* Startup founders and indie hackers
* Students learning to code with AI
* Anyone curious about AI-assisted development with Claude

You don’t need to be an expert—just a willingness to experiment and learn together.

### What to Bring

* A laptop
* A project, idea, or problem (optional but encouraged)
* Access to Claude (or just come observe and discuss)

Let’s spend our Saturdays building smarter, faster, and more thoughtfully—with Claude as our coding companion.
👉 **RSVP and join us for Claude Code Saturdays!**

name: Diffusion Paper Reading Group
description:
**Explore the Future of Generative AI: Diffusion Models Reading Group**
Are you passionate about the cutting edge of machine learning and generative AI? Join our **Diffusion Models Reading Group**—a community of enthusiasts, researchers, and practitioners dedicated to exploring the fascinating world of diffusion-based generative models.

**📅 When:** Every Wednessday
**🕒 Time:** 800 PM - 900 PM EST
**📍 Where:** Online in our discord server

**About the Group:** Diffusion models have revolutionized the field of generative AI, enabling breakthroughs in image synthesis, data generation, and more. Our reading group is designed to dive deep into the latest research, discuss foundational concepts, and explore innovative applications of diffusion models.

**Where?**

* Online meeting link to the discord server is released on the day of the meetup via announcement!

**What to Expect:**

* **Weekly Discussions:** Engage in insightful conversations about recent papers, breakthroughs, and methodologies in diffusion modeling.
* **Collaborative Learning:** Share your ideas, ask questions, and collaborate on projects with fellow members.

**Who Should Join:**

* **Machine Learning Enthusiasts:** Whether you're a student, researcher, or industry professional, if you're interested in generative models, this group is for you.
* **Data Scientists & Engineers:** Enhance your skills and stay updated with the latest advancements in AI.

**Discussion Notes from previous paper readings:**

* Here is our Google Doc where we take notes (I added edit privileges for the OBNYC mailing list, and comment privileges for anyone who has the link; let me know if you need me to give you edit privileges separately):
* [https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-HtbGd1lGj68YEmgv8Ekpg0aI7TUmQVpAbRp498AVj8/edit?usp=sharing](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-HtbGd1lGj68YEmgv8Ekpg0aI7TUmQVpAbRp498AVj8/edit?usp=sharing "https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-HtbGd1lGj68YEmgv8Ekpg0aI7TUmQVpAbRp498AVj8/edit?usp=sharing")

**See you there!**

name: AI Philly Hack Night
description:
Join us for AI Hack Night, a monthly gathering where AI builders, developers, and enthusiasts come together to present projects, share ideas, and collaborate on open source AI software. Whether you're showcasing your latest creation, seeking feedback, or looking for collaborators, this is your chance to connect with an engaged community of AI innovators. *Bring your laptop, ideas, and curiosity, and let’s build the future of AI together!*

**What to Expect:**

* **Discover New Tools:** Learn more about AI-apps like [Rork](https://rork.com/?ref=ai-philly).
* **Project Demos:** Share your AI project or get inspired by others.
* **Collaborative Feedback:** Share and receive constructive insights. Find support from peers.
* **Networking Opportunities:** Connect with like-minded builders and industry enthusiasts.

*AI Philly events are $5 to attend to help ensure we have an accurate RSVP count, engaged attendees, and pizza.*

name: 13th Philly Hardware Meetup @ BFTP!
description:
Join us for the 13th Philadelphia Hardware Meetup! Come join us to hear from our guest speakers as well as share what’s on your desk. We always have time for an open mic.

***[Please click here to RSVP on Luma!](https://luma.com/fvfwpcjy)***

​​​This time, we'll be at **Ben Franklin Technology Partners** new office at 1600 Market Street. Check in with security to be directed to the event space.
​
*Hardware Meetups happen across 30+ cities monthly and help bring together different professionals to connect and meet each other.*
​​
**We will have a great set of featured speakers for the evening - to be announced shortly!**

​**And pizza! Thanks to our host and sponsor, Ben Franklin Technology Partners. Drinks courtesy of Solid Product Design.**

​​​**Agenda**

* ​​​6:00-6:30pm: Arrive, mingle, grab some food and a beverage
* ​​​6:30-6:45pm: Opening remarks
* ​​​6:45-7:30pm: Guest Speakers + Q&A with the audience
* ​​​7:30-8:00pm: Open Mic. Anyone in attendance can give a demo or short presentation on their hardware product, startup, or project.
* ​​​8:00-8:30pm: Mix, mingle, and close out the night.

​​​
**Thank you to our partners:**

**​[Ben Franklin Technology Partners](https://benfranklin.org/)**, our food sponsor for the evening. BFTP creates and retains highly paid, sustainable jobs by investing in and linking early-stage technology firms and established manufacturers with experts, universities, follow-on funding, and other resources to help them prosper through innovation.

**[Solid Product Design](https://solidpd.com/)** is a Philadelphia-based hardware product design and development firm. We help companies ship products faster by combining deep research, first principles thinking, and a user-centered development approach. Our experienced team anticipates challenges early, helping clients identify and solve problems before they arise - so ideas move smoothly from concept to commercialization.

name: PLUG Central - Jitsi Meet: 7-9PM; Speaker from 7:30-8:30PM
description:
Please join us for PLUG Central. During the pandemic we meet on-line at Jitsi Meet, beginning at 7pm. RSVPs are optional. Anyone interested in Linux and Free and Open Source software is welcome to attend. We welcome all levels of expertise, whether you're just curious, a hobbyist, or a professional.

Jitsi Meet agenda:

Q&A with emcee CJ Fearnley from 7-7:30PM. Bring your Linux (and related) questions for a group exploration.

The main presentation from 7:30-8:30PM: TBD.

Q&A continued from 8:30-9PM.

After the pandemic dissipates, we plan to again meet in Lecture Hall A in Griffith Hall at USP in Philadelphia. We plan to revert to the original speaker slot from 8-9PM with Q&A at the beginning to better support travel constraints.

After the presentation some of us adjourn to Clarkville (http://www.clarkvillephilly.com/) a nearby pizza and craft beer restaurant.

University of the Sciences in Philadelphia (USP)
Griffith Hall (Room "A" or "C", look for the signs)
600 South 43rd Street
Philadelphia, PA 19104-4495

USP is located in University City. Driving directions are available at http://www.phillylinux.org/locations/usp.html, or http://www.usip.edu/directions, both of which have an aerial view of the campus buildings. USP is also easily accessible by public transportation.

Often, the door to Griffith Hall is locked at 7PM. If you find the door locked, please contact the security guard (usually in the booth in the parking lot) and tell them that the monthly PLUG (Philadelphia Linux User Group) meeting in Griffith Hall is tonight. If there is no one in the booth you can call UCS's public safety number at 215.895.1117 and they will radio personnel to unlock the door. Our sponsor is Zhiwei Liu in the Chemistry department. Security knows this and will almost always open the door for you and have it unlocked so other participants can enter without issue.
